choice: how about a nextjs app with a postgres backend? I was hoping to deploy it in railway.
rationale: Human-chosen during grooming (overrode recommendation: TypeScript / Node.js throughout, with the Monte Carlo core implemented as a pure-function library that can later be extracted into a worker thread or separate process if latency becomes a problem. Python (FastAPI + NumPy/SciPy) is faster for raw numeric throughput but adds a second language, a second deployment unit, and a cross-language serialization boundary. For a closed, low-concurrency tool the Node path is simpler and still fast enough — 10 000 paths × 40 years runs in well under a second in compiled TS with typed arrays.)
